underlying: Republican margin of victory, 2026 Kentucky U.S. Senate general election (Nov 3, 2026). OPEN seat — McConnell retiring.
method: Anchor: 2020 McConnell beat McGrath +19.6. 2022 Paul +23. 2024 Trump won KY by ~30pts. 2026 OPEN seat — McConnell retiring after 40 years. R primary Barr/Cameron/Morris. Likely D nominee Charles Booker (lost 2022 to Paul by ~23). Open seat + post-McConnell environment + D+5 generic ballot. Modeled R margin ~ N(14, 7).
notes: Open seat (McConnell retiring). KY governor Andy Beshear (D) twice elected statewide. Booker likely D nominee — lost 2022 to Paul by ~23 but better-funded now. R primary winner (Barr most likely from current polls) is establishment-aligned. 2024 Trump +30. Model: 14pts — closer to McConnell's 2020 result minus open-seat penalty and D environment penalty, partially offset by deepening R PVI. Kalshi YES asks: P12 0.73, P14 0.60, P16 0.47, P18 0.34 — market median ~15. We're roughly aligned.
